F42: Continual learning for AI agents is implemented through a three-phase pipeline: data distillation, knowledge consolidation, and experience replay.
In the data distillation phase of continual learning, a teacher model generates synthetic training examples that capture the distribution of production failures.
Knowledge consolidation phase of continual learning uses elastic weight consolidation.

Experience replay phase of continual learning maintains a buffer of 50,000 interactions.
Prioritized replay in experience replay phase of continual learning uses alpha=0.6.
The continual learning pipeline runs on a dedicated Kubernetes cluster with auto-scaling based on replay buffer utilization.
Each continual learning consolidation cycle takes 4 hours on 8 A100 GPUs.
The continual learning system checkpoints every 1,000 gradient steps to enable rollback.

F44: RAG metrics use a proprietary algorithm called RAGScore.
RAGScore combines information retrieval theory with attention patterns.
RAGScore tokenizes the retrieved context and response using a custom BPE tokenizer with a vocabulary of 128,000 tokens.
A 7B parameter model called RAGEmbed generates embeddings for RAG evaluation.
The retrieval quality matrix is computed using cosine similarity between document embeddings generated by RAGEmbed.
Context precision in RAGScore is the ratio of relevant to total tokens, weighted by their TF-IDF scores.
Context recall in RAGScore uses a modified version of ROUGE-L that accounts for semantic similarity rather than exact lexical overlap.

Faithfulness in RAGScore is measured by extracting knowledge triples from both the context and response using a relation extraction model, then computing the Jaccard similarity between the two triple sets.
The entire RAG evaluation pipeline runs in under 200ms per example on a single V100 GPU.
RAGScore results are calibrated against a reference dataset of 50,000 human judgments collected through Amazon Mechanical Turk.
